title: Cannes Film Festival: Cristian Mungiu wins Palme d'Or for 'Fjord' at closing ceremony overshadowed by the state of the world
subhead: On May 23, the jury, chaired by South Korean filmmaker Park Chan-wook, awarded the Romanian director with the Palme d'Or for the second time, and presented its Grand Prix to 'Minotaur' by the Russian director Andrey Zvyagintsev.

title: Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
Missile strikes pound Kyiv after Russia vows retaliation
Gunman killed by US Secret Service after opening fire near White House
subhead: President Donald Trump was inside the White House on Saturday evening when a gunman approached the building's west side and began firing. Secret Service Police returned fire, striking a bystander as well as the suspect, who was later pronounced deceased.

title: Trump says agreement with Iran 'largely negotiated' and includes opening strait
subhead: US President Donald Trump announced on Saturday that the proposal included opening the crucial Strait of Hormuz, though 'final aspects and details' were still being finalized.

title: Google executive James Manyika criticizes France for sidestepping AI: 'It's terrible for the French user'
subhead: Google's senior vice president of technology and society regrets that the group's innovations are not being rolled out in France, a country he considers too resistant to change. He hopes to reassure the public about the risks artificial intelligence poses to jobs.

title: Swedish transhumanist Nick Bostrom fears a 'pendulum swinging too far' against AI
subhead: The futurologist warns of the threat artificial intelligence poses to white-collar workers and the unprecedented existential crisis it could spark among the public, while also acknowledging its contributions to research and health care.

Tamil cinema superstar Vijay makes spectacular political debut in India
subhead: Actor Joseph Vijay Chandrasekhar has stunned the country by winning regional elections in Tamil Nadu. The 51-year-old relied on his millions of fans and campaigned on a platform of change.

title: What we know about the Paris brawl involving Nice supporters ahead of French Cup final
subhead: A major brawl broke out in Paris's 10th arrondissement, where around 100 supporters of OGC Nice, who are set to face RC Lens in the French Cup final, were gathered on Thursday evening. Sixty-five people were arrested.
byline: Antoine Albertini,  Laura Motet and  Sofia Fischer

title: Pakistan's powerful military leader Asim Munir is reshaping the country
subhead: In the year since Pakistan's brief conflict with India in May 2025, the head of Pakistan's armed forces has used the war to consolidate his hold on the state, further erasing the distinction between civilian and military power.
